title: Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards: government response to report
description: The government's response to the Committee's 2020 report Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards
summary: The government has released a policy document responding to a report on artificial intelligence and public standards, outlining potential actions to address concerns raised in the report. The document provides an account of the government's plans to ensure the safe and responsible development of artificial intelligence.

title: Report and impact assessment on Copyright and Artificial Intelligence
description: A report and impact assessment on the use of copyright works in the development of artificial intelligence (AI) systems.
summary: This report assesses the impact of using copyrighted works in the creation of artificial intelligence systems. Its goal is to consider the implications for copyright law when AI relies on copyrighted material.

title: Generative artificial intelligence (AI) in education
description: The Department for Education’s (DfE) position on the use of generative AI in the education sector.
summary: The Department for Education's policy on generative AI in education aims to guide teachers and institutions on the use of this technology in the classroom. The policy will help ensure that generative AI is used safely and effectively to support student learning and achievement.

title: Transport artificial intelligence action plan
description: Sets out how DfT will work with the transport sector to exploit the opportunities from AI while managing potential risks.
summary: The Transport Artificial Intelligence Action Plan outlines how to harness the benefits of AI technology in the transport sector while addressing potential risks. This plan aims to work closely with the transport sector to achieve a balance between innovation and safety.

title: Copyright and artificial intelligence: progress report
description: A progress report on the use of copyright works in the development of AI systems, under Section 137 of the Data (Use and Access) Act.
summary: This report aims to track the use of copyrighted materials in the development of artificial intelligence systems, highlighting any potential issues under current laws. The report focuses on Section 137 of the Data (Use and Access) Act, providing updates on the intersection of copyright and AI technology.

title: The regulation of artificial intelligence as a medical device: government response to the RHC
description: Government response to the recommendations made by the Regulatory Horizons Council (RHC) on the regulation of AI as a medical device.
summary: This policy document outlines the government's response to recommendations on regulating artificial intelligence as a medical device. The document aims to provide a framework for the safe and effective use of AI in medical settings.

title: ​​Ofqual’s approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence in the qualifications sector​
description: Published in response to a request from the Secretary of State for Education and the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology.
summary: Ofqual, the qualifications regulator, has set out its approach to managing the use of artificial intelligence (AI) in the qualifications sector. The document outlines how Ofqual will regulate AI to ensure it is used fairly and safely in exams and assessments.

title: Government response to the House of Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence
description: Government response to the Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence report ‘AI in the UK: No Room for Complacency’.
summary: The UK government has responded to a report by the House of Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence, aiming to address the potential risks and benefits of AI technology in the country. This response outlines the government's plans for managing AI development and ensuring its safe and beneficial use in the UK.

title: AI and Public Standards – Terms of Reference
description: Terms of Reference and FAQs for the Committee's review into artificial intelligence and public standards.
summary: The AI policy document outlines the terms of reference for a committee reviewing artificial intelligence and its relationship to public standards. The review aims to provide a framework for evaluating AI's impact on societal norms and values.

title: CSPL submission to Science and Technology Committee inquiry on Governance of AI
description: CSPL has submitted written evidence to the Science and Technology Committee's inquiry on Governance of Artificial Intelligence.
summary: The Cabinet Office's Security and Policy Committee has submitted their views on regulating Artificial Intelligence to an official government committee. This submission aims to inform the committee's inquiry into governance of AI.
